goal: Add agent hooks subsystem (cursor v1) + webhook dispatcher for domain events

summary.title: Agent hooks subsystem (cursor v1) + domain-event webhook bus
summary.what: Add an opt-in mainline hooks subsystem that integrates with native agent runtime hooks (Cursor today; Codex/Claude Code reserved) so the mainline flow auto-fires at session/turn boundaries, plus a webhook subscription mechanism that emits typed domain events to external HTTP endpoints with HMAC signing and detached-subprocess delivery.
summary.why: Today agents drive the mainline flow purely from 'memory': start, append, seal must be triggered manually each turn, which is brittle for long sessions and trivially forgotten. Hooks make the contract hard-to-skip rather than best-effort. Separately, external tooling (notification centers, dashboards, audit pipelines) had no integration surface short of scraping the actor log; typed webhooks give them a small, privacy-friendly subscription model. Both are opt-in and composable with the existing CLI workflow.
summary.user_goal: Implement a hook mechanism in mainline supporting cursor/codex/claude-code with extensibility, automate the mainline workflow inside agent runtimes to reduce reliance on agent memory, and add a webhook mechanism for external tools (notification center, etc.) with all-domain-events scope and prompt-content excluded.

decisions.chose: Opt-in installation via 'mainline hooks install <agent>'; soft-disable via 'mainline hooks disable' that toggles config without touching the agent's hooks.json
decisions.rationale: Friendlier for users with an existing CLI flow; avoids surprise edits to .cursor/; respects agents that co-own hooks.json.

decisions.chose: Read-modify-write with stable key ordering and a managed-marker discriminator so mainline-owned entries are added/removed cleanly without touching user-managed entries
decisions.rationale: User and mainline co-own the file; idempotency is required to make 'install' safe to re-run and to support clean uninstall.
rejected: Sidecar file then merge at agent startup

decisions.chose: Fail-soft sh -c wrapper that probes 'command -v mainline' and exec's the real subcommand only when the binary exists, exiting 0 otherwise
decisions.rationale: A missing or broken mainline binary must never break the agent runtime — automation is best-effort.

decisions.chose: Auto-run 'mainline seal --prepare' (writes seal.json next to the worktree); do NOT auto-submit the seal
decisions.rationale: The seal payload requires semantic judgment (fingerprint / risks / follow-ups) that hooks cannot fabricate; preparing is mechanical, submitting must remain explicit.

decisions.chose: engine.Service exposes a narrow EventBus interface (SetBus + nil-safe emit); webhook.Bus implements both engine.EventBus and hooks.EventEmitter; engine.HookFacade adapts engine.Service to the narrow surface hooks.Dispatcher needs
decisions.rationale: Avoids a hooks→engine→hooks import cycle, lets the engine emit events without importing webhook, and keeps both subsystems testable in isolation.

decisions.chose: Fire-and-forget detached subprocess: foreground CLI serializes an Envelope into .ml-cache/webhook-queue/, then forks 'mainline __webhook-dispatch' with syscall.Setsid (Unix) / DETACHED_PROCESS (Windows)
decisions.rationale: Foreground CLI must never block on HTTP, retries, or DNS; this pattern is a standard fire-and-forget approach for non-blocking telemetry-style sends.

decisions.chose: HMAC-SHA256 per subscription, X-Mainline-Signature: sha256=<hex> header; secret value supports $ENV:VAR expansion
decisions.rationale: Signed payloads let subscribers reject forgeries; env-var expansion keeps secrets out of committed config.toml.

decisions.chose: Domain events ONLY — no prompt content, no turn descriptions are exfiltrated through webhooks; this is enforced at the bus boundary, not just policy
decisions.rationale: User explicitly asked for all-domain-events but no prompt leakage; making it a code invariant is safer than a docs-only rule.
rejected: Pass full event payload including prompts

decisions.chose: Cursor ships v1; Codex and Claude Code packages exist under internal/hooks/agents/ but do NOT register with the registry — they document the extension contract for the next iteration
decisions.rationale: User asked to ship cursor first and reserve the others; scaffolded-but-unregistered packages give us a clear extension point without shipping half-finished agents.
rejected: Ship all three agents in v1
Single hard-coded cursor implementation with no extension point

decisions.chose: Bump agents-stub.md template v5→v6 to match agents-md.md v6, document the auto-flow contract in both, add README sections for hooks + webhook acknowledgements
decisions.rationale: Stub powers .cursor/rules/, CLAUDE.md, .windsurfrules, .github/copilot-instructions.md — all need the hooks contract; README discoverability and an explicit acknowledgement section cover open-source hygiene.
rejected: No template bump (would leave the auto-flow contract undocumented for downstream users)

rejected.reason: Surprise edits to .cursor/ are user-hostile; opt-in respects users who already have a working CLI flow.
The seal payload demands semantic judgment that hooks cannot synthesize; staging seal.json is the right level of automation.

rejected.alternative: Storing webhook secrets in committed config.toml
rejected.reason: Secrets must not be committed; supported $ENV:VAR expansion keeps them in the environment.
Would block the foreground CLI on slow / failing endpoints; detached subprocess pattern keeps the foreground responsive.

rejected.alternative: Including prompt content in webhook payloads
rejected.reason: Privacy boundary; user explicitly asked to exclude prompts.

rejected.alternative: Implementing all three agents (cursor / codex / claudecode) in v1
rejected.reason: User asked to ship cursor first and reserve the others; scaffolded packages provide the extension contract without shipping unfinished work.

rejected.alternative: A separate 'mainline-hookd' daemon for hook dispatch
rejected.reason: Extra moving part, lifecycle management headache; using the same mainline binary with a hidden __webhook-dispatch subcommand is simpler and more discoverable.

risks: Cursor hooks.json schema is observed empirically and can change between Cursor versions; install layer needs revisiting if Cursor introduces breaking changes to hook command shape or supported event names.
session_end's auto seal --prepare can produce stale seal.json files if the agent abandons mid-session; users may need a 'mainline seal --discard' or similar cleanup path eventually.

Detached subprocess pattern relies on syscall.Setsid / DETACHED_PROCESS; if a hostile parent process traps SIGCHLD or closes stdio aggressively the sender could still get killed. Acceptable for a best-effort notification path but documented.
HMAC secrets in environment variables can leak through process listings if not guarded by the OS; teams with stricter security postures may want a secrets-manager integration in a follow-up.

Phase1 conflict detection runs over the new domain-event payloads only AT seal/sync time; webhook subscribers do NOT see the same fingerprint dimensions yet, so dashboards built on webhooks may need a follow-up event type for fingerprint deltas.

EventBus emit() is fire-and-forget at the engine layer too — if Bus enqueueing itself fails (e.g. disk full), the engine action still succeeds. Errors are logged but not surfaced. Acceptable for v1 but documented.
followups: Implement the codex agent (internal/hooks/agents/codex) once Codex stabilizes its hook contract; the package skeleton is already present.
Implement the claudecode agent (internal/hooks/agents/claudecode) once we settle on whether to wrap the existing claude-code lifecycle hooks or use stdin-driven event JSON.
Surface webhook delivery failures in 'mainline status' so users notice persistent .failed.json buildup without a separate 'mainline webhook retry' invocation.
Add a 'mainline hooks status' health check that probes the agent's hooks.json for managed entries that no longer match the current schema (drift detection).
Consider extending the dispatcher to surface conflict warnings in stderr with a colour escape so they stand out in agent UIs that show stderr inline.
Add fingerprint-delta to the domain-event taxonomy so webhook subscribers can build phase1 dashboards without scraping the actor log.

architectural_claims: engine.Service depends on a narrow EventBus interface; the concrete webhook.Bus is the only implementation but engine never imports webhook.
internal/hooks depends on engine through engine.HookFacade (adapter), not through engine.Service directly; this avoids a hooks->engine->hooks import cycle and keeps hooks substitutable.
Webhook delivery is asynchronous and out-of-process; foreground CLI commands return as soon as the envelope is on disk and the detached subprocess has been forked.
Hook installation is an idempotent read-modify-write over .cursor/hooks.json; the file is co-owned with the user and only mainline-managed entries are touched.
Agent lifecycle events are normalized into a single Event taxonomy (session_start/turn_start/turn_end/session_end/subagent_*/compaction) so the dispatcher logic is agent-agnostic.
TeamConfig HooksSection has 5 independent toggles (enabled, auto_start_intent, auto_append_turn, auto_seal_prepare, auto_sync_on_session_start) so users can dial automation level per-toggle.
WebhookSubscription declares an event-type filter so subscribers receive only events they want; envelope-time filtering keeps the queue lean.
Webhook payloads carry domain events only — prompt content is enforced never-emitted at the bus boundary.
behavioral_changes: When 'mainline hooks install cursor' has been run, opening a Cursor session triggers 'mainline sync' automatically and surfaces conflicts on stderr before the first turn.
Each agent turn now auto-records a turn via 'mainline append' if hooks are installed and auto_append_turn is enabled.
Closing a Cursor session triggers 'mainline seal --prepare' automatically, leaving seal.json on disk for the agent to fill in and submit.
engine.Service now publishes typed domain events at the success boundary of Start, Append, SealSubmit, Sync, CheckSubmit; they are no-ops if no Bus is attached.
Sealing now also emits intent_sealed and conflict_detected events to the bus.
Sync now emits sync_completed and conflict_detected events, providing a webhook signal for newly detected phase1 overlaps.

api_changes.signature: mainline hooks enable | mainline hooks disable
mainline hooks <agent> <event>  (agent-facing dispatch endpoint, stdin payload)
mainline webhook list | remove <id> | test <id> | retry
hooks.Agent interface { Name(); DisplayName(); HookNames(); Install(); Uninstall(); IsInstalled(); ParseEvent() }
Event{Type=session_start|turn_start|turn_end|session_end|subagent_start|subagent_end|compaction, Agent, SessionID, Prompt(local-only), Summary, ModifiedFiles, Status, Reason, Raw}
webhook.Bus implements engine.EventBus + hooks.EventEmitter; engine.Service.SetBus injection seam
engine.HookFacade adapter exposes narrow engine surface to hooks consumers

data_model_changes.name: TeamConfig.HooksSection
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Defaults backfilled by storage.ReadTeamConfig when [hooks] is missing; older config.toml files keep loading unchanged.

data_model_changes.name: TeamConfig.Webhooks ([]WebhookSubscription)
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Empty slice when [[webhooks]] not present; no migration.

data_model_changes.name: webhook.Envelope (queue message schema)
data_model_changes.migration_notes: On-disk envelopes are versioned implicitly by file name; future schema bumps will move to a v2/ subdirectory.

data_model_changes.name: .ml-cache/webhook-queue/ + .ml-cache/hooks/ directories
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Created on demand by storage.Store; covered by existing .ml-cache/ gitignore rule.

security_implications: Webhook payloads are HMAC-SHA256 signed using a per-subscription secret; subscribers must verify the X-Mainline-Signature header to reject forgeries.
Secrets support $ENV:VAR expansion so they don't have to be committed in config.toml.
Hook command wrappers use sh -c with a fail-soft guard; the wrapper does not allow arbitrary user input to land in shell context (only managed event names).
Webhook payloads exclude prompt content by design, reducing accidental exfiltration of sensitive user prompts to third-party endpoints.
The detached __webhook-dispatch subprocess inherits no agent stdio; it gets its own session via setsid so it cannot leak stdout into the agent UI.
migration_notes: Existing repos: no migration required. Older config.toml files without [hooks] keep working — defaults backfilled at read time. agents-stub.md template bumped from v5->v6 so next 'mainline init' / setup will rewrite the wrapped block in CLAUDE.md / .windsurfrules / .github/copilot-instructions.md / .cursor/rules/mainline.md (user content outside markers preserved).

Users wanting automation: run 'mainline hooks install cursor' (or future agents) once per repo. Soft-disable via 'mainline hooks disable' is config-toggle-only and does not touch the agent's hooks.json.
Existing users with handwritten .cursor/hooks.json: install is read-modify-write and only adds the entries owned by the managed marker; user-managed entries are untouched.
